annotations.label: Method of exhaustion

anchor.snippet: if the two sequences eventually came closer together than any prespecified amount, then Q is found, or exhausted , by U and L

annotations.note: Mathlib contains unrelated 'exhaustion' concepts (sigma-compact spaces, measure-theoretic exhaustion sequences) but no formalization of Archimedes' historical method of exhaustion as a proof technique.

annotations.label: Cavalieri's principle

anchor.snippet: two volume with equal-area cross-sections are equal

annotations.note: Mathlib's layer cake formula is labeled 'Cavalieri's principle' but states the tail-probability/integral identity, not the geometric equal-cross-section-volume statement.

annotations.label: Problems solved by the Method

anchor.snippet: calculating the center of gravity of a solid hemisphere

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Finset.centroid` for finite point sets but does not formalize the center of mass of a solid hemisphere.

annotations.label: Bounds for surface area of a sphere

anchor.snippet: he gives upper and lower bounds for the surface area of a sphere by cutting the sphere into sections of equal width

annotations.note: Mathlib computes `EuclideanSpace.volume_ball` for the volume of Euclidean balls but has no surface-area-of-a-sphere formalization to bound.

annotations.label: Volume of a cylindrical wedge

anchor.snippet: A problem solved exclusively in the Method is the calculation of the volume of a cylindrical wedge

annotations.note: No grep hits for cylindrical wedge volumes in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Volume of intersection of two cylinders (Archimedean globe)

anchor.snippet: an announced result concerned the volume of the intersection of two cylinders

annotations.note: Mathlib has no formalization of the Steinmetz-solid / bicylinder intersection volume.

annotations.label: First proposition of Ostomachion (board of two squares)

anchor.snippet: the first proposition sets up a board consisting of two squares side by side

annotations.note: No Mathlib content on the Stomachion/Ostomachion puzzle.

annotations.label: Number of Suter-board Stomachion solutions

anchor.snippet: the number of ways to place the pieces of the Suter board to reform their square, allowing them to be turned over, is 17,152

annotations.note: The 17,152 Stomachion-tilings count is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Three packings of the Codex board

anchor.snippet: For the Codex board (again as with Tangram) there are three ways to pack the pieces

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Stomachion/Tangram packing enumerations.
